stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 15 observations with mean 41.0 % (within-factory SD = 7.0). factory B has 10 observations with mean 49.1 % (within-factory SD = 8.9). factory C has 20 observations with mean 53.0 % (within-factory SD = 9.3). factory D has 10 observations with mean 40.9 % (within-factory SD = 9.5). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.2 and 15.5 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 41.2 % (shrunk from raw 41.0). Range [41.1, 41.5] as between-factory SD varies from 6.2 to 15.5. Pooled mean: 46.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 46.8 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=10.3: B = 0.97.
Interval [41.1, 41.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (41.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (46.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
